title: campanula rotundifolia var. major
intro: Campanula rotundifolia var. major, also known as the tall bellflower, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic properties, and may help to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Campanula rotundifolia var. major can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve for topical use, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various health conditions.
